title: anthyllis vulneraria subvar. subunifoliata
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subvar. subunifoliata, also known as kidney vetch or mountain lucerne, is a subvariety of the kidney vetch plant, a perennial legume native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of kidney vetch subvar. subunifoliata include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation and pain, and support the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and can also be used topically as a poultice or applied as a salve to aid in wound healing.
